name: Garland Ranch Regional Park
attested_communities: Coast live oak woodland, Monterey pine forest, riparian vegetation along the Carmel River, grassland (high grassland near La Mesa). (Also redwood, Monterey cypress, and non-native eucalyptus — outside Trailside's 7-habitat vocab.)
note: The first four mapped habitats are explicitly named in the source.
curator_note: chaparral added by curator override (2026-06-30): chamise/manzanita chaparral occurs on Garland's drier upper slopes but is not named in the Wikipedia source. Flagged per Phase 1 provenance policy (never edit silently).

name: Asilomar State Beach
attested_communities: Rocky coast and dune habitat on the Monterey Peninsula; a trail running through the dunes; adjacent forest.
note: Dune/strand and forest are directly attested; 'coastal-bluff scrub' is mapped from the source's 'rocky coast' habitat and is a reasonable but inferred call.

name: Point Lobos State Natural Reserve
attested_communities: Cypress-covered headlands (one of only two native Monterey cypress stands), wildflowers, rich coastal flora.
note: Forest (Monterey cypress/pine headlands) and a coastal setting are attested; the specific 'coastal-bluff scrub' community is inferred from the coastal headland description. 'the headlands' is treated as an alias for Point Lobos.
